today.name: CREOSOTE
today.nextAction: v0.4.27 — VU METERS NOW READ THE OUTPUT + real ballistics. ROOT CAUSE (user: output differs between two shots but the VU reads the same): the VUs were fed summingLevel, tapped AFTER the summing amp but BEFORE EQ and the limiter. LIMIT is downstream of that tap, so pulling it from 0 to -18 dB moved the real output ~18 dB while the summing bus never changed — the needles were faithfully showing a point in the chain that did not move. FIX: the atomics are now outputRmsL/R, accumulated at the FINAL OUTPUT (post EQ/clip/limiter/master-bypass — literally what leaves the plugin), which is what a POWER OUTPUT meter must show. Still RMS, still metering-only. BALLISTICS also fixed: a VU hits 99pct of a step in 300ms = ~4.6 tau => tau 65ms => alpha 0.40 at 30Hz. v0.4.26 used 0.10 = tau 316ms = ~1.5s to settle, far too sluggish. Now 0.40 rise / 0.36 fall (near-symmetric, like a real movement). 0 VU stays aligned to -18 dBFS RMS. VERIFY IN ABLETON: move LIMIT and the needles must now follow the output; needles should settle in about a third of a second, not crawl. NOTE: a loud master will pin the needles near +3 — that is authentic VU behaviour; the -18 dBFS alignment is one constant in McIntoshVuMeter if you want them resting lower. OPEN: painted-metal chassis fork; deferred patina surfaces.
